codeReview.summary: Feature adds a single 657-line test file (src/app/api/__tests__/cross-area-user-journey.test.ts) with 39 integration tests covering XSS sanitization across 7 API routes, EXIF stripping consistency, bilingual translation completeness, URL-based locale routing, empty states, returning user data APIs, mobile responsive patterns, public gallery access, error boundaries, and full journey route structure. The worker also performed 12 interactive browser checks covering the full user journey in both languages, mobile viewport, and guest access. All validators passed (496 tests, typecheck clean, lint clean). The tests use a mix of runtime assertions (importing and testing actual functions like sanitizeInput, stripExifMetadata, routing config) and static-analysis-style assertions (reading source files as strings and checking for patterns). The static-analysis approach is somewhat brittle but adequate for the verification scope of this feature. No blocking issues found.

issues.description: VAL-CROSS-027/028/029 tests set up vi.doMock for @/lib/db and @/lib/auth but never actually invoke the route handlers. The mocks are dead code — the tests only verify file existence via fs.existsSync. This is misleading: the describe block is titled 'Returning user data persistence' but no data persistence is actually tested at runtime. The file existence checks are still valid structural verification, but the mocks should either be used (by importing and calling the route handler) or removed to avoid confusion.

XSS route coverage tests read source files as strings and check for 'sanitizeInput' substring. While this works, it could produce false positives (e.g., a comment mentioning sanitizeInput without actual usage). A more robust approach would be to import the route handler, call it with XSS payloads, and verify the output is sanitized. However, this is acceptable given the verification nature of this feature and the fact that other test files already test route handlers directly.

Mobile responsiveness test (VAL-CROSS-040) checks for Tailwind responsive class patterns (sm:|md:|lg:|max-w-) via regex on source code. This verifies responsive classes exist in the source but doesn't verify they produce correct layouts. The agent-browser interactive checks (mobile viewport at 375x812) provide the actual runtime verification, making this test a useful but non-essential structural check.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The containsSuspiciousPatterns function is exported from src/lib/sanitize.ts (line 29) but the AGENTS.md explicitly says 'do NOT gate sanitization behind containsSuspiciousPatterns() checks'. No API route currently uses it as a gate, and the cross-area tests verify this. However, the function is still exported and available — a future worker could accidentally use it. Consider either removing the export or adding a JSDoc warning.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/lib/sanitize.ts:29 exports containsSuspiciousPatterns. AGENTS.md Security section says 'Always call sanitizeInput() — do NOT gate sanitization behind containsSuspiciousPatterns() checks'. Tests at cross-area-user-journey.test.ts:56-57 verify routes don't use it.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker's test file placement (src/app/api/__tests__/cross-area-user-journey.test.ts) places cross-area tests under the api directory, even though many tests verify page components, translations, and layout concerns. A more conventional location might be src/__tests__/ or a dedicated integration tests directory, but this is not documented in AGENTS.md and the current placement doesn't cause issues.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Test file at src/app/api/__tests__/cross-area-user-journey.test.ts contains tests for page routes, translations, error boundaries, and responsive layout — concerns that span beyond API routes.

summary: PASS. The cross-area-user-journey feature delivers a comprehensive verification test suite (39 tests) and thorough interactive browser validation (12 checks). All expected behaviors from the feature spec are covered: XSS sanitization across all API routes, EXIF stripping consistency, bilingual translation completeness (EN/ID), locale routing, empty states for first-time users, returning user data endpoints, mobile responsiveness, public gallery access, error boundaries, and full journey structure. The worker followed the skill procedure correctly: ran baseline tests first, wrote tests, fixed a minor issue (unused import), ran all validators, and performed extensive agent-browser verification. Three non-blocking issues were identified: (1) dead mock code in returning-user tests, (2) string-matching approach for XSS verification is somewhat brittle, (3) responsive class checks are structural rather than visual. None of these prevent the feature from fulfilling its validation requirements.
